assets.description: Side-by-side PowerShell (left, primary) and Bash (right) panel showing the 7 exchanges: print home path, write note.txt, read it with a relative path, read it with an absolute path, rename to note.md, read the renamed file, then list the directory with dotfiles revealed

PNG render of the same 7-exchange side-by-side panel. The PowerShell column uses Get-Location, Rename-Item, and Get-ChildItem -Force; the Bash column uses pwd, mv, and ls -a. The note strip under each window summarises how that shell treats dotfiles and the -Force or -a flag
Typewriter animation of the 7-exchange PowerShell-primary walkthrough. Rendered with a 48-colour adaptive palette and typing_step=2 to stay well under the 2 MB gate

PowerShell-first screen-reader transcript with the full 7-exchange session (home path, write, read relative, read absolute, rename extension, read renamed, reveal dotfiles) plus the cross-shell takeaway explaining the PowerShell-attribute vs Bash-dot convention difference
